zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Felton於1308年出生在英國諾圖波藍德時，她的父親William de Felton52歲，母親Constance Pontop45歲。她於1328年6月4日之前與Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1338年之後在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Felton于1308年出生在英国諾圖波藍德时，她的父亲William de Felton52岁，母亲Constance Pontop45岁。她于1328年6月4日之前与Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1338年之后在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Felton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places so called. Most of them, including those in Herefordshire, Shropshire, and Somerset (Winford), are named from Old English feld ‘pasture, open country’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. Another place of the same name in Somerset, also known as Whitchurch, has as its first element Old English filethe ‘hay’. Felton Hill in Northumberland is named with the Old English personal name Fygla (a derivative of fugol ‘bird’; compare Fowle ).

variants.name: Fowle
meanings: English (Kent): patronymic from the Middle English personal name Foghel, Fughel (Old English Fugel, Fugol, from Old English fugol ‘fowl, bird’). Alternatively, a nickname from Middle English fughel, foghel, foul ‘fowl, bird’, either with reference to physical appearance, size, or behavior, or possibly also a nickname for someone who kept or sold birds.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Felton was born in 1308, in Northumberland, England, her father, William de Felton, was 52 and her mother, Constance Pontop, was 45. She married Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal before 4 June 1328. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died after 1338, in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Felton est née en 1308, à Northumberland, Angleterre, son père, William de Felton, avait 52 ans et sa mère, Constance Pontop, avait 45 ans. Elle a épousé Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal avant 4 juin 1328. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée après 1338, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Felton 1308 in Northumberland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Felton 52 und ihre Mutter Constance Pontop 45. Sie hat vor 4. Juni 1328 Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb nach 1338 in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Felton è nata il nel 1308 nel Northumberland, England, sua padre, William de Felton, aveva 52 anni e sua madre, Constance Pontop, aveva 45 anni. Lei sposato Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal prima del 4 giugno 1328. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1338, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Feltonが1308年にイギリスノースウンバーランドで生まれた時，父William de Feltonは52歳，母Constance Pontopは45歳であった。1328年6月4日以前にRobert Bertram Baron of Bothalと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1338年以後に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Felton(이)가 1308년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Felton(은)는 52세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance Pontop(은)는 45세였다. 그녀는 1328년 6월 4일 이전 Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1338년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Felton nasceu em 1308, em Northumberland, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Felton, tinha 52 anos e sua mãe, Constance Pontop, tinha 45 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal antes 4 de junho de 1328. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu após 1338, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Felton родилась в 1308 г. в местности нортумбирланд, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Felton было 52 года, а матери, которую звали Constance Pontop, 45. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal перед 4 июнь 1328 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла после 1338 г. в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Felton nació en 1308, en Northumberland, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Felton, tenía 52 años y su mamá, Constance Pontop, tenía 45 años. Se casó con Robert Bertram Baron of Bothal antes de 4 de junio de 1328. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1338, en su pueblo.
